pi_items.source: Web API check (e.g. wttr.in) for Amman real-world coordinates and verifier.py constants for tolerance limit.
setup_task.sh injects the table "pipelines" and registers it in gpkg_contents.
setup_task.sh inserts a feature into the "pipelines" table with name "Cairo-Baghdad".
verifier.py checks if n_points == 3 for the maximum score points.
setup_task.sh uses this path as GPKG_DEST to copy and modify the file, and export_result.sh reads from it.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated "pipelines" layer injected directly into the QField GeoPackage via setup_task.sh. This layer contains a single feature named "Cairo-Baghdad" representing a straight line between the cities. The task requires adding a vertex for Amman (lat: 31.95, lon: 35.93), which correctly matches the real-world coordinates for Amman, Jordan. The verifier explicitly enforces that the modified line has exactly 3 vertices, with the middle vertex located within 0.5 degrees of Amman.
